title: TherapyGym: Evaluating and Aligning Clinical Fidelity and Safety in Therapy Chatbots
abstract: TherapyGym is a framework for evaluating and improving therapy chatbots along two clinical pillars: CBT fidelity and safety. Fidelity is measured using a CTRS-based multi-turn scoring pipeline, while safety is assessed with therapy-specific risk labels. TherapyJudgeBench contains 116 dialogues with 1,270 expert ratings for auditing LLM judges against licensed clinicians. TherapyGym also uses CTRS and safety rewards for GRPO fine-tuning over simulated patient dialogues.
publication_status: ICLR 2026 OpenReview decision: Reject; arXiv preprint
quality_notes: All therapy dialogues are synthetic and the paper explicitly states the system is a research artifact, not clinical guidance or a deployment-ready therapy tool.
Official project page links to a GitHub repo that was public but empty when checked on 2026-06-07.
OpenReview reviewers raised concerns about simulated patient realism, annotation details, judge validity, reward hacking, and limited novelty of GRPO.
